by_judge.judge: C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO. 6 AT DHARWAD
D K SINGH & RAJESH RAI K
PRADEEP SINGH YERUR IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 01.06.2026.
PRADEEP SINGH YERUR IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 01.06.2026.

narrative:
On June 1, 2026, the Karnataka High Court achieved a 52.3% overall hearing rate, resolving 1,115 out of 2,133 scheduled cases across 36 judges. This performance reflects a notable increase in judicial efficiency compared to late April, where the court recorded lower overall hearing rates of 40.3% on April 28 (888 of 2,204 cases) and 35.0% on April 27 (1,082 of 3,089 cases). 

Individual judge portfolios revealed substantial caseload pressure. Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 managed the heaviest schedule of the day, successfully hearing 136 of 327 total listed cases across two sessions, translating to a combined 41.6% hearing rate. High-volume clearance was also led by Justice S Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19, who heard 97 out of 151 scheduled cases (64.2%), and Justice Suraj Govindaraj in Hall 10, who presided over 76 of 111 scheduled cases (68.5%). Sittings for Justice Pradeep Singh Yerur in Hall 13 were suspended, leaving 15 scheduled cases unheard.

In terms of procedural stages, preliminary stages demonstrated the highest conversion efficiency. Preliminary Hearings converted at 59.4% (224 of 377 cases) and Admissions reached 59.2% (186 of 314 cases), closely matched by Orders at 59.1% (309 of 523 cases). In contrast, Final Hearings lagged at a 41.6% conversion rate (143 of 344 cases). Writ Petitions remained the dominant case type, maintaining a stable hearing rate of 55.2% (464 of 841 cases), followed by Criminal Petitions at 50.0% (217 of 434 cases). Regular Second Appeals showed the strongest clearance efficiency among major categories, achieving a 72.4% hearing rate for its 105 scheduled cases.
